qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) proposals, and through which portal should they be submitted?
qa_pairs.answer: Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) proposals must be submitted electronically through the CCDB portal (https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/resource_allocation_competitions/applications) no later than November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: What is the requirement for the Canadian Common CV (CCV) in a Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application, and what are the consequences of not providing it?
qa_pairs.answer: An up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) is required for both the Principal Investigator (PI) and all Co-PIs as part of the peer review process for any Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) application. Failing to provide an updated CCV will negatively impact the overall score of the submitted application, and deadline extensions for missing CCVs are not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What are the benefits of attending a pre-submission consultation for the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC), and by when should new applicants schedule one?
qa_pairs.answer: Pre-submission consultations are strongly encouraged for first-time applicants to the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC) and should occur before October 31, 2025. The consultations help determine if a RAC application is justified, verify project eligibility for the correct process (RPP vs RRG), and provide technical assistance with calculating needed resources.
